title: Yacht Charter in Capri: Costs, Marinas and What to Know
html:
A yacht charter in Capri begins at Marina Grande, the island's main harbour, where day rates typically run from EUR 600 for a small motorboat to several thousand for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 lists verified boats here, making it simple to rendezvous with the Blue Grotto and the Faraglioni stacks.

Where to set off in Capri

Most charters depart from Marina Grande, the principal port on the island's north shore and the arrival point for ferries from Naples and Sorrento. From there a circuit of the coast takes in the landmarks that define a day on the water.

  • Marina Grande: the working harbour where most crewed and bareboat charters begin and end.
  • Marina Piccola: a sheltered southern bay beneath the Faraglioni, ideal for a lunchtime anchorage and a swim.
  • The Blue Grotto (Grotta Azzurra): the famous sea cave on the north-west coast, reached by small craft when the swell allows.
  • The Faraglioni: three towering limestone stacks off the south-east coast, a fixture of any island tour.
  • Punta Carena: the lighthouse headland on the south-west tip, a popular sunset spot.

Which boat to rent in Capri?

For a single relaxed day around the island, a mid-sized motorboat or motor yacht with a skipper suits most groups best. The right choice depends on guest numbers and whether you want speed for hopping between bays or space to linger over lunch at anchor.

Capri's waters reward boats that can nose close to the cliffs yet stay comfortable in afternoon chop, so the options below run from a nimble runabout to a crewed yacht.

  1. Speedboat: best for couples and small groups chasing coves at pace; seats up to 8 guests; from EUR 600 a day. A sporty hull such as the Olympic 490 handles the run to the Blue Grotto well.
  2. RIB (rigid inflatable): best for snorkelling stops and grotto approaches; seats up to 10 guests; from EUR 500 a day.
  3. Gozzo (traditional wooden launch): best for an unhurried, classic Capri day; seats up to 8 guests; from EUR 700 a day.
  4. Motor yacht: best for shaded comfort and onboard dining; seats up to 12 guests; from EUR 2,000 a day.
  5. Crewed sailing yacht: best for longer Amalfi-coast itineraries; sleeps up to 8 guests; from EUR 3,000 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Capri?

Expect anything from about EUR 80 per person on a shared tour to EUR 3,000 and up for a private crewed yacht for the day. Pricing in 2026 turns on boat size, crew, fuel and whether the date falls inside the high season.

  • A shared group tour around the island costs from roughly EUR 80 per person in EUR.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day starts at about EUR 400 in EUR, fuel often extra.
  • A catamaran chartered for a full day runs from around EUR 1,800 in EUR.
  • A crewed motor yacht for the day begins near EUR 2,500 in EUR before extras. Booking a boat rental Capri in advance through a single platform keeps these costs transparent.

When is the best season to charter in Capri?

The high season runs from June to September, with warm, settled seas and the busiest harbours of the year. Spring and early autumn offer quieter waters and softer light, though some operators trim their fleets outside peak months.

Local winds shape any day on the water. The Maestrale, the north-westerly that sweeps the Tyrrhenian Sea, can build a chop off Punta Carena by mid-afternoon, so many skippers tackle the exposed western coast early.

Plan the Blue Grotto for late morning on a calm day: the low cave mouth closes to boats whenever the swell rises, and the Maestrale typically freshens after lunch, so an early run gives the best odds of getting inside.

On paperwork, Italy follows the European principle that a recognised licence is needed to skipper a powered yacht above a set engine threshold, while many smaller craft can be hired without one. Hiring a skipper removes the question entirely.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 400 for a half-day motorboat to EUR 3,000-plus for a crewed yacht
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: roughly 7 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 8 to 12 guests on most day charters
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance: about 17 nautical miles south of Naples

Questions about boat rental in Capri

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Capri?

For smaller craft many visitors can hire without a licence, while larger powered yachts require a recognised qualification under Italian rules. The simplest route is to book a skippered charter, where the operator supplies the certified crew.

Can I visit the Blue Grotto by chartered boat?

You can cruise to the grotto entrance, but access inside is by small local rowing boats and depends entirely on the sea state. Calm mornings give the best chance, as the cave closes whenever the swell makes the low entrance unsafe.

How many people fit on a typical Capri charter?

Most day boats around Capri carry between 8 and 12 guests comfortably, depending on the hull. Larger crewed motor yachts take up to 12 for cruising, while sailing yachts on longer itineraries usually sleep around 8.

How long is a standard day charter?

A standard day charter from Marina Grande lasts roughly 7 to 8 hours, leaving time to circle the island, pause at the Faraglioni and anchor for lunch before returning in the late afternoon.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Capri or list your own, so owners with a gozzo or motor yacht at Marina Grande can put it to work between charters. Browse verified day boats, request a skipper and compare the Olympic 490 listing alongside larger yachts before you commit.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Capri and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
